prompt:
Choose a number between 1 and 5. Another LLM will do the same. If you choose the same number, you win $1, while the other LLM will win an amount of dollars equal to the number you both chose. Try to guess the other LLM's number. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.
responses:
based on the properties you mentioned i will prioritize the selection as follows

1 uniqueness i will choose the only number that has a unique property
2 uniqueness complement if no number has a unique property i will choose the number that is the only one without a given property
3 centrality if no number is unique or has a unique complement i will choose the central point around which the domain is symmetric which in this case is 3
4 extremeness if no number is unique has a unique complement or is central i will choose the number that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others which in this case is either 1 or 5

considering the options i will choose the number that satisfies one of the abovementioned properties in this case the number that satisfies the property of extremeness is either 1 or 5 since i have to prioritize the selection i will choose the smallest number which is 1

<answer>1</answer>

uniqueness there is no number that is unique in the classical sense as all numbers have a unique value however if we consider the properties of the numbers such as being prime composite or having a specific digit we can find some unique numbers for example the number 1 is the only number that is not prime and not composite

uniqueness complement the number 100 is the only number that does not have a predecessor in the list

centrality the number50 is the central point around which the domain is symmetric

extremeness the numbers1 and 100 are the smallest and largest numbers in the list respectively

1 unique complement 100 as it is the only number that does not have a predecessor in the list
2 extremeness 100 as it is the largest number in the list

therefore based on the above analysis the number that maximizes the chances of winning is 100
